title: Palo Alto Threat Intelligence Feed Freshness
spl:
index=pan sourcetype="pan:system" logdesc="*threat*feed*"
| stats latest(update_time) as ut by feed_name
| where now()-ut > 86400
description: Stale threat feeds reduce blocking efficacy for known malicious indicators.
value: Stale threat feeds reduce blocking efficacy for known malicious indicators.
implementation: Track dynamic update and EDL refresh times. Alert when feeds fall behind vendor SLA.
knownFalsePositives: Palo Alto matches from certificate-pinned clients, health checks, or SSL-decryption exception paths that are expected under your policy; verify against Panorama and change tickets.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_paloalto`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=pan:system`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Track dynamic update and EDL refresh times. Alert when feeds fall behind vendor SLA.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=pan sourcetype="pan:system" logdesc="*threat*feed*"
| stats latest(update_time) as ut by feed_name
| where now()-ut > 86400
```

Understanding this SPL

**Palo Alto Threat Intelligence Feed Freshness** — Stale threat feeds reduce blocking efficacy for known malicious indicators.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=pan:system`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_paloalto`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: pan; **sourcetype**: pan:system. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Detection type** for this use case: TTP — interpret thresholds and fields in that context.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=pan, sourcetype="pan:system". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by feed_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where now()-ut > 86400` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search over a window that includes both normal production traffic and, if you have one, a documented test (vendor sandbox, lab rule hit, or exercise). Compare a few sample events to the vendor’s own console or reports and tune indexes, fields, and thresholds so alerts are actionable before paging.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (stale feeds), Single value.
grandmaExplanation: We use Palo Alto Networks firewall data and alerts to stay ahead of the risks described in "Palo Alto Threat Intelligence Feed Freshness". We help the team notice trouble early, while a situation is still small and containable.
dataModelAcceleration: After vendor logs are CIM-tagged, enable Data Model Acceleration for the CIM models listed on this use case; otherwise the optional tstats path may return no rows.
